exemplar.title: How does the temperature (room temperature which is 20-25Â°C +/-1Â°C and refrigeration which is 4Â°C +/-1Â°C)  affect the ripening process of kiwifruit and vitamin C content as determined by percentage of ascorbic acid and citric acid levels using test strips, and what is the optimal ripening time for peak vitamin C levels?

detailedExemplarData.title: How does the temperature (room temperature which is 20-25Â°C +/-1Â°C and refrigeration which is 4Â°C +/-1Â°C)  affect the ripening process of kiwifruit and vitamin C content as determined by percentage of ascorbic acid and citric acid levels using test strips, and what is the optimal ripening time for peak vitamin C levels?

subject.title: Biology
subject.description:
**IB Biology** is a **Group 4** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **four interconnected themes: Unity and Diversity, Form and Function, Interaction and Interdependence, and Continuity and Change**, with a strong focus on **experimental work, scientific inquiry, data analysis, and practical investigation skills**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2025**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Multiple-choice and data-based questions | 1 hour 20 min | 2 hours | 36% |
| **Paper 2** | Short and extended response questions | 1 hour 30 min | 2 hours 30 min | 44% |
| **Scientific Investigation** | Individual research project | 10 hours | 10 hours | 20% |

strengths: The research question is narrowly focused, specifying organism, temperature conditions and response variable with clarity.
The introduction provides a specific and appropriate biological and industry context for studying vitamin C in kiwifruit.
The methodology description is detailed enough to allow reproduction, with clear step-by-step procedures and defined variables.
weaknesses: Some methodological considerations (e.g., impact of humidity control, test strip reliability) are not fully explained.
Ambiguities remain in sampling frequency and calibration procedures (humidity control, strip calibration).
Minor phrasing issues and an incomplete final statement of the aim reduce clarity.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The studentâs work clearly sits in the 5â6 band for research question context but in the 3â4 band for methodological considerations and methodology description. The context is specific and well articulated (justifying a near-top score), while methodological rationale and reproducibility have modest omissions. A holistic score of 5 reflects these strengths and minor gaps.

strengths: Data tables and multiple graphs demonstrate a clear organizational structure.
Appropriate statistical tests (t-tests, ANOVA, Tukey) are attempted, showing purposeful engagement with data analysis.
Means and standard deviations are reported, indicating an understanding of dispersion measures.
weaknesses: Instrumental uncertainties (test strip resolution, syringe tolerance, temperature variability) are not quantified.
Formatting inconsistencies (decimal separators, notation errors) and missing labels hinder precision and clarity.
Calculation and reporting errors (duplications, missing degrees of freedom, incomplete ANOVA table) reduce analytical rigor.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While the student communicates data recording and processing clearly or precisely in parts, there are significant omissions in uncertainty treatment and some inaccuracies in processing. This places the work solidly in the 3â4 band but closer to the lower end, warranting a score of 3.

strengths: The conclusion restates key numerical outcomes (significance for citric acid vs. ascorbic acid) and links them to p-values.
The conclusion is relevant to the research question and generally consistent with the analysis presented.
weaknesses: Overstatements (e.g., claiming full control of temperature effects) weaken precision.
The comparison to accepted scientific context is superficial and lacks critical depth.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The conclusion is relevant and mostly consistent with the analysis (3â4 band) but contains overgeneralizations and only a basic literature comparison. These factors justify a mid-band score of 3.

strengths: Specific methodological weaknesses (small sample size, limited duration, variable control issues) are identified.
Realistic improvements (e.g., using DCPIP titration, extending duration, increasing sample size) are linked to those weaknesses.
weaknesses: There is no explanation of the relative impact of each limitation on the results.
Improvements are described but not explained in terms of how much they would enhance precision or validity.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The evaluation describes specific weaknesses and realistic improvements (3â4 band) but stops short of explaining their relative impact or quantifying benefits. A holistic score of 3 accurately reflects this level of evaluation.

strandSelections.reasoning: The conclusion restates key numerical outcomes (significant for citric acid, not for ascorbic acid) and correctly links them to p-values, demonstrating **overall consistency with the analysis**.  Minor over-generalisations (e.g., âexperiment successfully adjusts for temperatureâ) keep it from the top band, so the higher end of 3-4 is appropriate (score 4).

Raw tables, processed means/SD, and multiple graphs are supplied.  Formatting glitches and missing image placeholders reduce clarity, but numerical data can still be followed.  The work is **clear OR precise, but not both simultaneously**, matching the 3-4 bandâs lower boundary (score 3).

Means, SDs, t-tests, ANOVA and Tukey post-hoc are attempted, showing purposeful processing.  Nonetheless, transcription errors (e.g., duplicated numbers), unclear notation and tiny sample size raise doubts about rigour, so the strand fits **âcarried out with some significant inaccuraciesâ** (score 3).

Specific weaknesses are identified: small sample size, short duration, limited control of other environmental variables, and low precision of test strips.  The student does not analyse the **relative impact** of each weakness, so the work is described rather than explainedâmid-band (score 3).

The student mentions earlier studies on storage temperature effects and briefly contrasts their findings with the current results.  The comparison is **relevant but superficial** (mainly descriptive, little critical depth), placing it in the middle of the 3-4 band (score 3).

The student formulates a focused question, clearly naming the organism, the two temperature conditions and the response variable (vitamin C content).  A full paragraph in the introduction explains why kiwifruit and vitamin C are of interest to consumers and industry, demonstrating a **specific and appropriate biological context** rather than a vague mention.  Minor phrasing issues do not detract from the contextualisation, hence placement near the bottom of the 5-6 band (score 5).

Only statistical dispersion (SD, SE) is treated; **instrumental uncertainty of the test strips, temperature tolerance, syringe volume error, etc., are absent**.  Hence evidence of uncertainty treatment is minimal, warranting the bottom of the 1-2 band (score 1).

Suggested improvementsâuse DCPIP titration, extend duration, increase sample sizeâare **realistic and clearly linked to the stated weaknesses**, but the reasoning stops short of explaining *how much* each change would enhance validity.  Hence a descriptive level within the 3-4 band (score 3).

The student identifies key variables (temperature, time, fruit variety, measurement technique) and states why controlled environments and test strips were chosen.  However, there is **little depth** on how each factor might influence accuracy, and the reliability limits of the strips are only touched upon in evaluation, not here.  Thus methodological considerations are **described**, not fully explained, matching the lower end of the 3-4 band (score 3).

Step-by-step instructions, quantities, temperatures and sampling frequency are provided, enabling another investigator to reproduce the study with modest inference.  Ambiguities remain (e.g., exact humidity control, calibration of strips, how fruits are randomised), so **reproducibility is possible but not flawless**.  This aligns with mid-band performance (score 3).

annotations.comment: Data collection section states use of test strips but does not specify strip resolution or uncertainty. Quantify instrumental uncertainty.
Raw data table uses image placeholders instead of numeric values, reducing clarity. Present actual recorded values for Day 1 and label units.
Only Day 1 data is shown in body; directing to appendix hinders clarity. Include a representative multiâday dataset here.
Table 1 uses overline on every Cell instead of mean notation only in header, causing confusion. Standardize notation of averages.
Table 2 mixes commas and periods as decimal separators and displays excessive significant figures, inconsistent with test strip precision. Align formatting to Â±2 sig figs.
Graphs lack axis labels, units, and clear legends. Ensure figures include fully labeled axes, error bars, and descriptive captions.
The t-test section uses incorrect notation such as $$X^{-1}=1.771\bar{X}$$. Present clear formulas: $$\bar X_1=no.\;$$ and define symbols.
Standard error, t-statistic, and p-value are listed but lack degrees of freedom or calculation steps. Include df and formula steps for transparency.
ANOVA section reports $$p<0.05$$ without Fâvalue, degrees of freedom, or sum of squares. Supply full ANOVA table for rigor.
Tukey post-hoc description is narrative only; specific pairwise comparisons and critical Q values are absent. Tabulate Tukey results.
Interpretation conflates impacts of storage and time without isolating effects. Clarify which factor drives each acidâs trend.
Qualitative data on firmness is anecdotal; implement a scoring system (e.g., penetrometer readings or ordinal scale) for systematic capture.

The research question clearly specifies organism, temperature conditions, and response variables, providing strong focus. Consider refining phrasing to more precisely define âoptimal ripening time.â
The introduction situates kiwifruit vitamin C importance well, demonstrating specific biological and industry context. To strengthen, include a citation for health benefit claims.
The final sentence of the aim paragraph is cut off, leaving the study objectives incomplete. Ensure the aim is fully stated for clarity and reproducibility.
The null hypothesis is clearly stated but does not differentiate ascorbic from citric acid responses. Specify each compoundâs expected behavior under Hâ.
The stated aim outlines conditions and duration but omits specification of sampling frequency precision. Add explicit sampling interval details (e.g., âevery 2 Â± 0.5 daysâ).
Independent and controlled variables are listed, but humidity control is mentioned later only. Integrate humidity as a controlled variable here with justification.
Apparatus list is comprehensive, but lacks specification on calibration of test strips and temperature sensors. Include calibration procedures for reproducibility.
Dividing only ten fruits into two groups may yield insufficient statistical power. Consider increasing sample size to at least fifteen per condition.
The procedure header is clear, but humidity control is not described. Detail how humidity was measured and maintained.
Sampling description states âevery two days for twelve days, at total for 7 days,â which is inconsistent. Clarify the duration and number of sampling points.
Syringe sampling volume Â±1 mL is too imprecise for a 1 mL target. Specify a tighter tolerance or method to minimize pipetting error.
Justification mentions controlled storage but omits test strip accuracy limitations. Add discussion of strip sensitivity and potential errors.
Conclusion states the method âsuccessfully adjusts for temperature,â which overstates control. Temper tone to reflect limitations.
Methodological weaknesses are identified (small sample size, short duration) but lack discussion of their relative impact on results. Quantify which factor most affects validity.
Suggestions include DCPIP titration but do not explain how much precision would improve statistical power. Link improvement to specific error reduction.
Scientific context comparison in conclusion relies on superficial reference to previous studies. Critically evaluate how your findings extend or contradict specific literature.
Bibliography includes multiple Wikipedia entries; consider replacing general sources with peerâreviewed articles to strengthen scientific credibility.

strands.content: Methodological considerations associated with collecting relevant and sufficient data to answer the research question are explained.

strands.name: Consideration of uncertainties

strands.name: Data processing quality
strands.content: The processing of data relevant to addressing the research question is carried out but with some significant omissions, inaccuracies or inconsistencies.

strands.name: Communication of data recording and processing
strands.content: The communication of the recording and processing of the data is both clear and precise.

strands.name: Consideration of uncertainties

strands.name: Data processing quality

strands.name: Scientific context comparison

strands.name: Conclusion relevance and consistency
strands.content: A conclusion is described that is relevant to the research question but is not fully consistent with the analysis presented.

strands.name: Scientific context comparison

strands.name: Conclusion relevance and consistency
strands.content: A conclusion is justified that is relevant to the research question and fully consistent with the analysis presented.

strands.name: Scientific context comparison
strands.content: A conclusion is justified through relevant comparison to the accepted scientific context.

strands.name: Improvements

strands.name: Methodological weaknesses

strands.name: Improvements
strands.content: Realistic improvements to the investigation, that are relevant to the identified weaknesses or limitations, are explained.
